title: 16 Creating a Regular Wire
body_text:
16	Creating a Regular Wire
16.1	Overview of Creating Regular Outgoing Wires
Follow your financial institution芒聙聶s policies and procedures for preparing wires before entering them in the Wires application. See Chapter 14, Considerations for Outgoing Wires, and Chapter 15, Preparing Outgoing Wires, for additional information.
To create a regular outgoing wire:
Log in to Wires as described in Chapter 10, Logging in to Wires.
Select
Create Regular
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select 芒聙聹Create Regular芒聙聺
16.2	Completing the Sending Financial Institution Section
Complete the Sending Financial Institution section as follows:
Select the sending branch from the Branch drop-down menu. See Figure 2. If you have access to one branch, the sending branch will default. Central 1 debits the branch that you select.
Note: 	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Figure 2: Select the Sending Branch
16.3	Completing the Beneficiary芒聙聶s Financial Institution Section
Complete the Beneficiary芒聙聶s Financial Institution section as follows:
Enter the routing instructions (i.e., SWIFT code, transit number, or ABA number) for the receiving financial institution in the 芒聙聹Swift/Transit #/etc.芒聙聺 field and select
Search
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Search for the Beneficiary芒聙聶s Financial Institution
Alternatively, select
Advanced Search
to search for the receiving financial institution using the name and address. See Figure 3. Enter the applicable criteria and select
Search
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Advanced Search for Beneficiary Financial Institution
Select the appropriate financial institution from the search results. See Figure 5. The beneficiary financial institution芒聙聶s information automatically populates in Wires.
Figure 5: Select the Beneficiary芒聙聶s Financial Institution
If the destination financial institution芒聙聶s country is listed as a prohibited, tax haven / high risk, or a sanctioned country, a warning message displays. See Figure 6. Warning messages are provided on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed.
Figure 6: Warning Message
Note:	If the destination FI芒聙聶s country is listed as a prohibited country, an error message displays after selecting 芒聙聹Next芒聙聺, and the wire won芒聙聶t be permitted to proceed. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Prohibited Country Warning and Error Message
16.4	Completing the Wire Amount Section
Complete the Wire Amount section as follows. See Figure 8.
芒聭
Enter the value of the wire in the Amount field.
芒聭隆
Select the wire currency. The currencies available in the Currency drop-down menu vary based on the beneficiary financial institution.
芒聭垄
Enter the sender芒聙聶s fee in the Fee field. If your financial institution has configured pre-populated fees in the Wires application, the Fee field and the fee currency automatically populate. The fee amount is editable.
Important
: 	The sender芒聙聶s fee is the outgoing wire fee that your financial institution charges your customer for the wire. Central 1 charges your financial institution a processing fee per wire which is charged to your Central 1 account at month end. To recover your processing costs, ensure the fee that you charge your customers (pre-populated or manually entered) is, at minimum, the value of the Central 1 processing fee. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
Note:	For current fees, Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. For other Central 1 clients, contact your Account Executive.
Figure 8: Wire in CAD Currency
US Dollar Wires
To create a US dollar wire:
Complete the Amount, Currency, and Fee fields as previously described. The currency defaults to US dollars if the beneficiary financial institution芒聙聶s country is the United States. See Figure 9.
Select the applicable value from the 芒聙聹Sender paying in芒聙聺 drop-down menu. If you select
USD 芒聙聯 United States Dollar
, the transaction is charged in US dollars.
Figure 9: US Dollar Wire 芒聙聹Sender Paying in芒聙聺 Currency
Note:	When sending US dollar wires to another Wires participating financial institution, a US correspondent bank isn芒聙聶t required, the wire is routed to the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s pending incoming wires.
Foreign Currency Wires
Foreign exchange rates apply if the value in the Currency drop-down menu isn芒聙聶t Canadian or US dollars.
To create a foreign currency wire:
Complete the Amount, Currency, and Fee fields as previously described. Foreign currency wire fees are in Canadian dollars.
An estimated exchange rate displays. Wires determines the final exchange rate when you complete the transaction. See Section 16.16, Confirming a Foreign Exchange Quote.
Note:	The exchange rate includes the Central 1 rate and, if applicable, your financial institutions芒聙聶 Foreign Exchange (FX) spread. See Chapter 12, Managing Foreign Exchange Spreads in Wires.
Optionally, enter the Canadian dollar value of the wire in the CAD Equivalent Amount field to calculate the estimated foreign currency value. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Wires in Foreign Currency
16.5	Completing Intermediary Information
If you are sending a wire that isn芒聙聶t in the domestic currency of the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s country (e.g., sending GBP to France), the Intermediary Financial Institution field displays. Wires may also require an intermediary based on the beneficiary financial institution.
Sending Canadian Dollar Wires to the US
You must obtain the intermediary information from the sender when sending Canadian dollars to the US. Record the intermediary information on the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171.
You must search for and select the specified intermediary when creating the wire on the Wires application. Central 1 can芒聙聶t provide the intermediary information. To search for an intermediary:
Select
Search by Intermediary FI Name/Address
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Selecting 芒聙聹Search by Intermediary FI Name/Address芒聙聺
Enter the intermediary that the sender provides, then select
Search
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Entering Search Criteria
Select the appropriate financial institution from the search results. The intermediary financial institution displays in Wires. See Figure 13. Alternatively, select
Clear
to remove the intermediary.
Figure 13: Selected Intermediary Financial Institution
Sending
Wires in the Non-domestic Currency of the Receiving Financial Institution芒聙聶s Country (e.g., Euros to the United Kingdom)
Select an intermediary from the Intermediary Financial Institution drop-down menu. Central 1 recommends selecting an intermediary from the top of the list. The recommended intermediary is the default. See Figure 14. The intermediary defaults if a single intermediary is available.
Figure 14: Selecting an Intermediary Financial Institution
Note:	Wires doesn芒聙聶t require users to select an intermediary when sending a wire in the domestic currency of the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s country (e.g., GBP to the United Kingdom). Central 1芒聙聶s correspondent bank facilitates the routing of the wire to the receiving financial institution.
Note:	All international outgoing wires are processed through intermediary financial institution(s) and/or a correspondent financial institution that can deduct fees from the wire. Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t control the fees other financial institutions charge.
16.6	Completing the Wire Priority Section
The Wire Priority section displays if the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution is in Canada and the wire currency is Canadian dollars. The high-priority wire option only applies to wires destined for other high-value system (Lynx) participant financial institutions (e.g., Royal Bank of Canada, Bank of Montreal), not to credit unions or other Central 1 clients. If you select the high-priority option, Lynx processes the wire with a higher priority. See Section 1.5, Contacts, Websites, and other Resources, for a link to the other Payments Canada high-value system (Lynx) participants. Additional processing fees apply to high-priority wires.
Important
: 	Authorized Wires Users must approve a wire before Lynx processes it as a high-priority wire.
Complete the Wire Priority section as follows (see Figure 15):
Select
Regular
to send a regular priority Canadian dollar domestic wire (Regular is the default).
Select
High
to send a high-priority Canadian dollar domestic wire.
Figure 15: Completing the Wire Priority Section
If your financial institution has configured pre-populated fees in the Wires application, Wires automatically includes your add-on fee in the Fee field when users create a high-priority wire.
16.7	Completing the Future Dated Wire Section
The Future Dated Wire section displays if the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution is in Canada and the wire currency is Canadian dollars. The Future Date field allows users to schedule the release of a wire on a specified date. Authorized users must approve future-dated wires prior to the scheduled date, or Central 1 converts the wire to a regular wire. Additional processing fees apply to future-dated wires.
Important
: 	Once you approve a futured-dated wire, you can only cancel it by submitting a trace request to Central 1. Central 1 must receive requests to cancel a future-dated wire no later than 2:15 pm PT / 11:15 am ET the business day before the specified value date (future date). A cancellation fee applies. Cancellation requests are processed on a best- efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. If the wire was sent to the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution on the value date, you must initiate a recall request. See Chapter 25, Recalling, Tracing, and Amending Wires.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Complete the Future Dated Wire section as follows. See Figure 16.
Leave blank if you芒聙聶re not creating a future-dated wire.
Select a date using the Future Date calendar widget to create a future-dated wire. You can select a date up to 14 days from the current date.
Figure 16: Completing the Wire Priority Section
If your financial institution has configured pre-populated fees in the Wires application, Wires automatically includes your add-on fee in the Fee field when users create a future-dated wire.
16.8	Completing the Sender Section
Complete the Sender section as follows:
Select Person or Business/Entity as applicable and enter the sender芒聙聶s details. Fields with an asterisk (*) are required. See Figure 17.
Note:	The Sender芒聙聶s address must be a physical address. PO Boxes aren芒聙聶t permitted.
Figure 17: Enter the Sender芒聙聶s Information
A warning message displays if the sender芒聙聶s or third party芒聙聶s country of residence is listed as a prohibited, tax haven / high risk, or sanctioned country. Warning messages are provided on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Warning Message
Wires may prompt you to enter additional information about the Sender for FINTRAC reporting purposes if it determines that the outgoing wire may be reportable on a FINTRAC EFT Report. See Figure 19.
Note:	If you have customized Wires to always display FINTRAC fields, enter the additional information for every outgoing wire. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
Complete the FINTRAC additional details fields if the information is available to you. Reasonable efforts must be taken to complete the fields.
Figure 19: Additional Sender Information Required for FINTRAC Reporting Purposes
Note:	The text in the message box beside the FINTRAC additional information fields is customizable. See Chapter 4, Customizing Wires Elements.
芒聢聮
If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, the information that you enter is included in the data file and report file that Central 1 provides your financial institution.
芒聢聮
If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t participate in the Wires Data Extract Service, users can print a wire receipt to retrieve the information that you enter in Wires.
Important
:	Your financial institution is responsible for the analysis of data collected in Wires. If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, you are responsible for reporting outgoing wires to FINTRAC. Your financial institution can appoint an AML service provider to report wires to FINTRAC on your behalf. FINTRAC reporting isn芒聙聶t provided through the Wires application.
16.9	Completing Third-Party Sender Information
A third-party question displays when you芒聙聶re sending a wire to a financial institution outside of Canada in a foreign currency. See Figure 20. Select a response to the following:
Is the sender creating this wire on behalf of someone else?
芒聢聮
Select
Yes
or
No
. If you select Yes, the Sender 3rd Party Details section displays.
Figure 20: 3rd Party Sender Question
Complete the Sender 3rd Party Details section if applicable. See Figure 21. Different fields will display if the sender type selected is 芒聙聹Person芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Business/Entity芒聙聺.
Figure 21: 3rd Party Sender Section
16.10	Completing Representative/Conductor Details Section (Customizable Element)
The Representative/Conductor Details section collects additional information about the individual conducting the wire. The Representative/Conductor section displays if you have customized the Wires application to enable the feature and you select 芒聙聹Business/Entity芒聙聺 as the sender type when creating an outgoing wire.
Complete the Representative/Conductor Details section. See Figure 22. Wires may prompt you to enter additional information about the representative/conductor for FINTRAC reporting purposes if it determines that the outgoing wire may be reportable on a FINTRAC EFT Report.
Note:	If you have customized Wires to always display FINTRAC fields, the additional information is collected for every outgoing wire. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
Complete the FINTRAC additional details fields for the representative/conductor of the wire if the information is available to you. Reasonable efforts must be made to complete the fields. See Figure 27. The information you enter is available as follows:
芒聢聮
If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, the information recorded is included in the data file and report file that Central 1 provides your financial institution.
芒聢聮
If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t participate in the Wires Data Extract Service, you can print the wire receipt to retrieve the information recorded in Wires.
Figure 22: Complete the Conductor Details Section
Note:	The text in the message box beside the FINTRAC additional information fields is customizable. See Chapter 4, Customizing Wires Elements.
Important
:	Your financial institution is responsible for analysis of the data collected on Wires. If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, you are responsible for reporting outgoing wires to FINTRAC. Your financial institution can appoint an AML service provider to report wires to FINTRAC on your behalf. FINTRAC reporting isn芒聙聶t provided through the Wires application.
16.11	Completing the Beneficiary Section
Complete the Beneficiary section as follows:
Select
Person
or
Business/Entity
, as applicable, and enter the beneficiary芒聙聶s details. See Figure 23. The fields differ based on the selected option. Fields with an asterisk (*) are mandatory.
Note:	 The beneficiary芒聙聶s address must be a physical address. PO Boxes aren芒聙聶t permitted.
Figure 23: Beneficiary is a Person
Important
:	Accuracy of the account number is critical. If the receiving financial institution relies on the account number to credit the beneficiary, it has satisfied its obligations to the beneficiary even if the name on the account doesn芒聙聶t match the name in the payment message. Finality of payment may not apply if the individual involved in making the credit available to the beneficiary notices the discrepancy. Since this is difficult to prove, always ensure that the payment instructions are accurate. A recall can be requested but it芒聙聶s processed on a best-efforts basis and isn芒聙聶t guaranteed.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Enter an IBAN in the Account Number field if prompted. The user is prompted to enter an IBAN if the beneficiary芒聙聶s country of residence is an International Bank Account Number (IBAN) regulated country. See Figure 24.
Caution
:	Failure to include the correct IBAN can result in costly charges. The recipient bank can deduct fees from a wire if it must correct an IBAN. See Section 14.3, IBAN Requirements.
Figure 24: IBAN Prompt
Wires validates the length and starting characters of the IBAN. A warning displays if the data entered doesn芒聙聶t pass validation. See Figure 25. Wires also validates that the IBAN contains a valid check digit if it passes the first two validation points.
Figure 25: IBAN Warning
Important
:	Wires won芒聙聶t prevent a wire from being sent if an IBAN isn芒聙聶t entered or is entered incorrectly
.
A warning message displays if the beneficiary芒聙聶s country of residence is listed as a prohibited, tax haven / high risk, or sanctioned country. See Figure 26. Warning messages are provided on a best- efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed.
Figure 26: Warning Message
Wires may prompt you to enter additional information about the beneficiary for FINTRAC reporting purposes if it determines that the outgoing wire may be reportable on a FINTRAC EFT Report.
Note:
If you have customized Wires to always display FINTRAC fields, the additional information is collected for every outgoing wire. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
Complete the FINTRAC additional details fields if the information is available to you. Reasonable efforts must be taken to complete the fields. See Figure 27. The information you enter is available as follows:
芒聢聮
If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, the information recorded is included in the data file and report file that Central 1 provides your financial institution.
芒聢聮
If your financial institution doesn芒聙聶t participate in the Wires Data Extract Service, you can print the wire receipt to retrieve the information recorded in Wires.
Figure 27: Extra Beneficiary Information Required for FINTRAC Reporting
Note:	The text in the message box beside the FINTRAC additional information fields is customizable. See Chapter 4, Customizing Wires Elements.
Important
:	Your financial institution is responsible for analysis of the data collected on Wires. If your financial institution participates in the Wires Data Extract Service, you are responsible for reporting outgoing wires to FINTRAC. Your financial institution can appoint an AML service provider to report wires to FINTRAC on your behalf. FINTRAC reporting isn芒聙聶t provided through Wires.
16.12	Completing the Third-Party Beneficiary Information
A third-party question displays if the wire is being sent to a financial institution outside of Canada, in a foreign currency. See Figure 28. Select a response to the question as follows:
Is the beneficiary receiving this wire on behalf of someone else?
芒聢聮
Select
Yes
or
No
. If
Yes
, the Beneficiary 3rd Party Details section displays.
Figure 28: Third Party Beneficiary Question
Complete the Beneficiary 3rd Party Details section if applicable. See Figure 29. Different fields display if the beneficiary type is 芒聙聹Person芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹Business/Entity芒聙聺.
Figure 29: Beneficiary 3rd Party Details Section
16.13	Completing the Payment Details Section
Use the Payment Details section to record information for the beneficiary, (e.g., an invoice number) as well as additional information about the wire if required by the destination country (e.g., a purpose of payment code, identification information). The data you enter in the Payment Details section travels with the payment. This can improve the accuracy and completeness of payment instructions and reconciliation, automate post-payment activities, and enable better cash flow management. See the
Prohibited Remittance Content Procedure
for content that can芒聙聶t be included in payment messages.
Note:	If the beneficiary芒聙聶s country requires country-specific payment instructions that include 芒聙聹personal information芒聙聺 as defined in applicable privacy legislation, the sender must confirm that the disclosure of any personal information about an individual included in information accompanying the wire has been consented to by such individual.
Complete the Payment Details section as follows:
Enter information for the beneficiary in Lines 1芒聙聯4. See Figure 30.
Figure 30: Completing the Payment Details Section
A notification displays if there are country-specific guidelines, instructions, or payment code requirements for the destination country.
Select the link in the notification to download a PDF document that contains reference information on the country-specific requirements if available. See Figure 31.
Note:	See Section 14.4, Country-Specific Guidelines, Instructions, and Payment Codes.
Enter the purpose of payment code in Line 1. Enter more information about the wire in Lines 2芒聙聯4, if applicable. See Figure 31.
Figure 31: Country-Specific Requirements for a Wire
Note:	The wire can be sent without completing the Payment Details section, however the receiving financial institution may return the wire if there isn芒聙聶t enough information.
Related Information
Prohibited Remittance Content Procedure
16.14	Completing the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information Section
Use the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section to record additional details for the financial institution(s) involved in the wire. Completion of this section isn芒聙聶t mandatory.
Complete the Sender FI to Receiver FI Information section as follows:
Enter information for the receiving financial institution in Lines 1 and 2 (e.g., the type of payment) as appropriate. See Figure 32.
Figure 32: Sender FI to Receiver FI Information
16.15	Completing the Regulatory Section
Use the Regulatory section to record the following information about the wire. See Figure 33.
Responses to Politically Exposed Person (PEP) / Head of International Organization (HIO) determination questions.
芒聢聮
Displays if 芒聙聹Politically Exposed Person / Head of International Organization (PEP/HIO) Determination芒聙聺 is enabled for your financial institution. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
芒聢聮
Applies to wires greater than the equivalent of $100,000 Canadian.
If the transaction is face to face.
芒聢聮
Displays for all outgoing wires.
The payment method and source of funds.
芒聢聮
Displays if 芒聙聹Collect Funding Method芒聙聺 is enabled for your financial institution. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
芒聢聮
Displays for all outgoing wires.
Note:	You must comply with all regulatory requirements. This includes, but isn芒聙聶t limited to, record keeping, identification of the beneficiary, and FINTRAC reporting. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
Select
Yes
or
No
for each of the following PEP/HIO questions, if applicable:
芒聢聮
Has the sender, or any member of their family, now or at any time in the past, been employed as a senior official in a foreign state (芒聙聹Foreign PEP芒聙聺)?
芒聢聮
Has the sender, or any of their family members or close associates, now or at any time during the past five years, been employed as a senior official in Canada (芒聙聹Domestic PEP芒聙聺)?
芒聢聮
Is the sender, or any of their family members or close associates, currently employed as the head of an international organization or the head of an institution established by an international organization (芒聙聹HIO芒聙聺)?
Note:	The PEP declaration can be recorded on the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171.
Select
Yes
or
No
to the question 芒聙聹Is this transaction face to face?芒聙聺 This field displays for all wires and is mandatory. Follow FINTRAC regulations regarding non-face-to-face transactions.
Figure 33: Regulatory Section
Tip:	Hover your cursor over the question mark for additional information/requirements for regulatory questions.
Select a payment method from the 芒聙聹How is the sender paying for this wire?芒聙聺 drop-down menu, if applicable. See Figure 34.
Figure 34: Select a Payment Method
Select
Yes
or
No
to the question 芒聙聹Was information about the source (person/entity) of funds obtained?芒聙聺 If
Yes
, enter how the requester initially acquired the funds for the wire (e.g., sale of large asset, gift, employment income). Select
Next
when all applicable mandatory fields are complete. See Figure 35.
Figure 35: Select 芒聙聹Next芒聙聺 when Fields are Complete
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
Politically Exposed Person Statement,
Form 2351
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
16.16	Confirming a Foreign Exchange Quote
You are prompted to confirm a foreign exchange quote if the wire requires a foreign exchange rate. Foreign exchange doesn芒聙聶t apply for wires sent in Canadian or US Funds.
Note:	 If the wire doesn芒聙聶t require a foreign exchange rate, confirm the transaction as described in Section 16.17, Confirming the Wire Transaction.
Confirm a foreign exchange quote as follows:
Select
Confirm
when prompted to confirm the current foreign exchange quote or
Refresh
to get an updated quote. See Figure 36.
Note:	A countdown timer indicates when the foreign exchange quote will expire. If the quote expires, select
Refresh
to get a new quote.
Figure 36: Foreign Exchange Confirmation
16.17	Confirming the Wire Transaction
Have the customer verify that the wire data is correct before confirming the wire. You can print a copy of the wire confirmation for the sender to review and sign per financial institution policy.
To print a wire confirmation:
Select
Print Confirmation
. See Figure 37. See Figure 38 for a sample wire confirmation.
Figure 37: Print Confirmation
Figure 38: Sample Wire Confirmation
To confirm the wire transaction:
Select
Create Regular Wire Transfer
. See Figure 39. Alternatively, select
Edit
to make changes or select
Cancel
to cancel the creation of the wire.
Figure 39: Selecting 芒聙聹Create Regular Wire Transfer芒聙聺
Select
Print Receipt
to print a wire receipt for your customer. See Figure 40.
Note:	The wire reference number and number of approvals required displays when you complete the wire. All wires must be approved. See Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
Figure 40: Print Receipt
END
